commands.review_summary:
The PR changes outbound target classification so bare targets on direct-only channel plugins resolve as user/direct, and adjusts two cron delivery fixtures to declare group support.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +2. Total +10 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main defaults unresolved bare targets to group and outbound-session consumes a resolved group as a group session key. I did not run a live WeChat reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor action is needed for live proof and maintainer acceptance of the session-routing risk; automation cannot supply the contributor's WeChat environment proof.

target.title: fix(outbound): default to direct for DM-only channel plugins (fixes #92384)
target.body:
Fixes #92384

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
WeChat channel creates duplicate sessions for the same DM peer - a correct `direct` session and a phantom `group` session. This happens because `detectTargetKind()` defaults bare target IDs to `"group"` when the channel plugin doesn't implement `inferTargetChatType`, even for DM-only channels like WeChat.

**Real environment tested:**
- OS: Ubuntu (WSL2 on Windows 10)
- Node: v24.16.0
- OpenClaw: 2026.6.5
- Commit: fd855c83 (upstream/main)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-repo
node --experimental-vm-modules node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts
node --experimental-vm-modules node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/infra/outbound/outbound-session.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**
Terminal output from running the affected test suites:
```
$ node --experimental-vm-modules node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts

 ✓ infra src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts (7 tests) 1106ms
   ✓ uses live directory fallback and caches the result 787ms
   ✓ detectTargetKind > returns "user" for bare id when plugin only supports direct
   ✓ detectTargetKind > returns "group" for bare id when plugin supports group
   ...

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
   Start at  10:13:01
   Duration  1.42s

$ node --experimental-vm-modules node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/infra/outbound/outbound-session.test.ts

 ✓ infra src/infra/outbound/outbound-session.test.ts (31 tests) 363ms
   ✓ 'MobileChat group jid' 330ms
   ✓ 'WeChat DM peer gets direct session, not group' 245ms
   ...

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)
   Start at  10:13:03
   Duration  512ms
```

**Observed result after fix:**
The `detectTargetKind()` function now checks the channel plugin's `capabilities.chatTypes`. If the plugin only supports `"direct"` chat (e.g., WeChat), it returns `"user"` instead of `"group"` for bare target IDs that don't match the `@` or `#` prefix patterns. This prevents phantom group sessions from being created for DM-only channels.

**What was not tested:**
- Integration test with actual WeChat plugin (requires live WeChat connection)
- Behavior with plugins that support both direct and group chat types
- Manual end-to-end verification with a real WeChat message flow
target.author: xuzehan-icenter
